content.problemStatement:
Given a singly linked list, determine if it contains a cycle/loop. A loop exists when the last node's next pointer points to any previous node in the list instead of NULL.

Note: Update the `detectLoop` with your solution


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN, the number of nodes
Second line contains NNN integers denoting the values of the nodes
Third line contains pos, the position (1-based) where the last node connects to (0 if no loop)




Output Format

For each test case, print "YES" if a loop exists, "NO" otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1:

Last node points to second node (3), forming a loop

Test case 2:

Last node points to NULL, no loop exists

Test case 3:

Last node points to first node (1), forming a loop
